title: Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund Direct Growth
content:
Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund Direct Growth is an actively managed equity mutual fund that invests primarily in mid-cap stocks listed on Indian stock exchanges. The fund aims to generate long-term capital appreciation by identifying mid-cap companies with strong earnings growth potential. Category: Equity - Mid Cap. Sub-category: Open Ended Equity Scheme. Risk Level: Very High. Minimum Investment: Rs 500 for lump sum, Rs 500 for SIP (Systematic Investment Plan). The fund is suitable for investors with a long-term investment horizon of 5 years or more who can tolerate high volatility in exchange for potential higher returns.

Expense Ratio and Fees: The total expense ratio (TER) for the direct plan is 0.58% per annum. This means for every Rs 10,000 invested, approximately Rs 58 per year is deducted as the fund management and operating fee. Direct plans have significantly lower expense ratios compared to regular plans (which typically charge 1.5% to 2.0%) because direct plans eliminate distributor commissions. The expense ratio covers fund manager fees, administrative costs, registrar fees, audit fees, and other operating expenses of the fund. The expense ratio is charged daily on the net assets of the fund and is reflected in the NAV.

Exit Load: An exit load of 1% applies if you redeem (sell) or switch out your units within 15 days from the date of allotment. If you hold for more than 15 days, there is no exit load. Exit load is charged to discourage very short-term trading and to protect long-term investors from the costs imposed by frequent redemptions.

Fund Manager and Strategy: Managed by Niket Shah and Santosh Singh. The fund follows a focused bottom-up stock selection approach, targeting 25 to 30 high-conviction mid-cap stocks. The portfolio concentrates on businesses with strong competitive advantages and high earnings growth visibility over a 3 to 5 year horizon.

Performance: The fund has delivered strong returns over the long term. Investors should note that past performance does not guarantee future results, and mid-cap funds carry higher risk than large-cap funds due to greater price volatility.

title: Motilal Oswal Flexi Cap Fund Direct Growth
content:
Motilal Oswal Flexi Cap Fund Direct Growth is an actively managed diversified equity mutual fund that invests across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks without any fixed allocation constraint. The fund has the flexibility to move between market capitalizations based on prevailing valuations and opportunities. Category: Equity - Flexi Cap. Sub-category: Open Ended Dynamic Equity Scheme. Risk Level: Very High. Minimum Investment: Rs 500 for lump sum, Rs 500 for SIP.

Expense Ratio and Fees: The total expense ratio (TER) for the direct plan is 0.64% per annum. For every Rs 10,000 invested, approximately Rs 64 per year is charged as the management fee. Flexi cap funds typically carry slightly higher expense ratios than pure index funds because of active stock selection across multiple market caps. Compared to the regular plan (which can charge 1.6% to 2.1%), the direct plan offers a meaningful cost advantage over time due to the absence of distributor commissions. The expense ratio is deducted daily from the fund's net assets and reflected in the NAV.

Exit Load: An exit load of 1% is applicable if units are redeemed or switched out within 15 days of allotment. After 15 days, no exit load is charged.

Fund Manager and Strategy: Managed by Niket Shah. The fund uses a focused portfolio approach with 25 to 35 high-conviction stocks selected using bottom-up fundamental analysis. The manager looks for businesses with superior earnings growth potential and strong return on equity across all market segments.

Suitability: Suitable for investors seeking diversified equity exposure with flexibility across market caps and a long-term investment horizon of 5 or more years.

title: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund Direct Growth
content:
Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund Direct Growth is a passively managed index fund that tracks the Nifty Midcap 150 Index, which represents the top 150 mid-cap companies listed on NSE by free-float market capitalization. Category: Index Funds / ETF. Sub-category: Open Ended Scheme Replicating Nifty Midcap 150 Index. Risk Level: Very High. Minimum Investment: Rs 500 for lump sum, Rs 500 for SIP.

Expense Ratio and Fees: The total expense ratio (TER) for this index fund is approximately 0.20% per annum, which is significantly lower than actively managed mid-cap funds. For every Rs 10,000 invested, only about Rs 20 per year is charged as the expense ratio. This low cost is a key advantage of index funds because there is no active stock selection, and the fund simply replicates the index composition. The expense ratio covers index licensing fees, fund administration, custodian fees, and audit costs. The cost differential between this index fund (0.20%) and an actively managed mid-cap fund (0.58% to 0.75%) can meaningfully impact long-term wealth creation through the compounding effect.

Exit Load: An exit load of 0.1% applies if you redeem units within 15 days from the date of allotment. After 15 days, there is no exit load. The exit load on index funds is typically very low because index investing is intended for long-term holding.

Tracking Error: The fund aims to minimize tracking error relative to the Nifty Midcap 150 Index. A lower tracking error indicates the fund closely mirrors the index performance.

Active vs Passive Comparison: Index funds like this fund charge much lower expense ratios than active funds because there is no fund manager making discretionary stock picks. For cost-conscious investors, index funds offer market returns at minimal cost. However, they cannot outperform the index, while active funds have the potential (but not certainty) to beat the benchmark.

title: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund Direct Growth
content:
HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund Direct Growth is an actively managed equity mutual fund that invests at least 35% each in large-cap and mid-cap stocks as mandated by SEBI. This category ensures balanced exposure to both the stability of large-cap companies and the growth potential of mid-cap companies. Category: Equity - Large & Mid Cap. Sub-category: Open Ended Equity Scheme. Risk Level: Very High. Minimum Investment: Rs 100 for lump sum, Rs 100 for SIP.

Expense Ratio and Fees: The total expense ratio (TER) for the direct plan is 0.82% per annum. For every Rs 10,000 invested, approximately Rs 82 per year is charged as the expense ratio. The regular plan of this fund charges approximately 1.7% to 1.85%, so the direct plan offers a cost saving of roughly 1% per year. Over a long investment period, this compounded cost saving can significantly improve net returns. The expense ratio includes the fund manager's fees, administrative expenses, custodian charges, and other operational costs.

Exit Load: An exit load of 1% applies if you redeem units within 1 year from the date of allotment. After 1 year, there is no exit load. The 1-year exit load window is longer than some other funds and is designed to encourage investors to stay invested for the medium term.

Fund Manager and Strategy: Managed by Gopal Agrawal. The fund follows a blend of top-down sector allocation and bottom-up stock selection. The large-cap portion provides stability, while the mid-cap portion is selected for higher growth potential.

Suitability: Ideal for investors who want broad equity exposure across market caps with a medium to long investment horizon of 3 to 5 years or more, and who are comfortable with equity market volatility.

title: HDFC Flexi Cap Direct Plan Growth
content:
HDFC Flexi Cap Direct Plan Growth (historically known as HDFC Equity Fund) is one of India's oldest and most established equity mutual funds. It is a flexibly allocated fund that invests across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks based on the fund manager's market outlook and bottom-up stock selection. Category: Equity - Flexi Cap. Sub-category: Open Ended Dynamic Equity Scheme. Risk Level: Very High. Minimum Investment: Rs 100 for lump sum, Rs 100 for SIP.

Expense Ratio and Fees: The total expense ratio (TER) for the direct plan is 0.76% per annum. For every Rs 10,000 invested, approximately Rs 76 per year is charged. The regular plan charges approximately 1.7% to 1.8%, making the direct plan approximately 1% cheaper annually. The long track record of this fund means historical performance data is available over multiple market cycles. Expense ratios for flexi cap funds are higher than index funds but lower than funds with more concentrated mandates. The expense ratio directly reduces the NAV of the fund each day.

Exit Load: An exit load of 1% is applicable if units are redeemed within 1 year of allotment. After 1 year, no exit load applies.

URL Note: Despite being renamed to Flexi Cap Fund, this fund continues to use the legacy URL slug hdfc-equity-fund-direct-growth on Groww. Always use the Groww URL from the canonical resources list rather than constructing it from the current scheme name.

Fund Manager and Strategy: Managed by Roshi Jain. The fund actively selects stocks across market capitalizations with a focus on quality businesses at reasonable valuations. Portfolio is typically 50 to 60 stocks.

Long-term Track Record: One of India's oldest actively managed equity funds with a history spanning multiple decades, providing data across bull and bear market cycles.

title: HDFC Large Cap Fund Direct Growth
content:
HDFC Large Cap Fund Direct Growth is an actively managed equity fund that invests at least 80% of its assets in large-cap stocks (top 100 companies by market capitalization in India). Large-cap funds are generally considered lower risk than mid-cap or small-cap funds due to the established nature and financial stability of large-cap companies. Category: Equity - Large Cap. Sub-category: Open Ended Equity Scheme. Risk Level: High. Minimum Investment: Rs 100 for lump sum, Rs 100 for SIP.

Expense Ratio and Fees: The total expense ratio (TER) for the direct plan is 0.75% per annum. For every Rs 10,000 invested, approximately Rs 75 per year is charged as the expense ratio. The regular plan charges approximately 1.65% to 1.75%. The direct plan saves investors roughly 0.9% to 1.0% per year, which compounds to a significant difference over a long investment period. Large-cap active funds tend to have lower expense ratios than mid-cap active funds, though still higher than index funds tracking similar benchmarks like Nifty 50 or Sensex.

Exit Load: An exit load of 1% is charged if units are redeemed within 1 year from the date of allotment. After 1 year, there is no exit load.

Expense Ratio Context for Large-Cap Funds: Since large-cap funds invest in the top 100 stocks (which are widely researched and tracked), there is ongoing academic and industry debate about whether active large-cap funds can consistently outperform low-cost index funds after accounting for the expense ratio. Investors comparing this fund against Nifty 50 or Nifty 100 index funds should factor in the expense ratio difference (0.75% vs 0.10% to 0.20% for index funds).

Fund Manager: Managed by Gopal Agrawal. Portfolio typically holds 40 to 50 large-cap stocks selected for quality, earnings visibility, and reasonable valuations.

title: Mutual Fund Fees Explained: Expense Ratio and Exit Load
content:
Understanding mutual fund fees is essential for evaluating investment costs and comparing funds. The two main fees investors encounter are the expense ratio and the exit load.

Expense Ratio: The expense ratio (also called Total Expense Ratio or TER) is the annual fee charged by the mutual fund to cover its operating costs, including fund manager fees, administrative expenses, custodian charges, registrar fees, and marketing costs. It is expressed as a percentage of the fund's average net assets. For example, an expense ratio of 0.75% means Rs 75 per year is charged on every Rs 10,000 of investment. The expense ratio is not a one-time charge; it is deducted daily from the fund's NAV. SEBI has set limits on maximum expense ratios. For equity funds above Rs 10,000 crore AUM, the maximum TER for regular plans is 1.05%, and for direct plans is 0.80%.

Direct vs Regular Plan Expense Ratio: Direct plans have lower expense ratios than regular plans because direct plans are purchased directly from the AMC without going through a distributor, eliminating the distributor commission (typically 0.5% to 1.5% per year). Over a 10 to 20 year investment period, this cost difference can result in 15% to 30% more corpus in a direct plan compared to a regular plan.

Exit Load: Exit load is a fee charged when you redeem (sell) your mutual fund units within a specified period after purchase. It is designed to discourage short-term trading and protect long-term investors from the redemption costs caused by frequent transactions. Exit load is expressed as a percentage of the redemption amount. For example, a 1% exit load on Rs 10,000 redemption would result in Rs 100 being deducted. Many funds waive exit load after 1 year. Some short-term or index funds have exit loads only for very short holding periods (15 days) with no load after that.

How to Compare Funds by Fee: When comparing mutual funds, always look at both the expense ratio and the exit load together. A fund with a lower expense ratio will outperform an identical fund with a higher ratio over time, all else being equal. Use Groww's comparison tools to see the expense ratio and exit load side by side for any fund.
